findings: java/temporal-review/src/main/java/com/utm/temporal/agent/CodeQualityAgent.java @@ -29,10 +29,10 @@ public class CodeQualityAgent { | "System.getenv().getOrDefault(\"OPENAI_MODEL\", \"gpt-4o-mini\")" — This hardcodes a new default model in the agent, while the shared client still defines its own default. That creates duplicated configuration and a hidden coupling point; apply Extract Constant or Introduce Parameter Object so the model default comes from one source of truth.

java/temporal-review/src/main/java/com/utm/temporal/agent/SecurityAgent.java @@ -29,10 +29,10 @@ public class SecurityAgent { | "System.getenv().getOrDefault(\"OPENAI_MODEL\", \"gpt-4o-mini\")" — Same duplicated default-model logic as the other agents. This is repeated configuration that will drift over time; apply Extract Constant and pass the model through a shared configuration boundary.

java/temporal-review/src/main/java/com/utm/temporal/agent/TestQualityAgent.java @@ -31,7 +30,7 @@ public class TestQualityAgent { | "public AgentResult analyze(String prTitle, String prDescription, String diff, TestSummary testSummary)" — The method signature was simplified by removing the learning-context parameter, but the diff does not show the call sites. Not enough context in diff to assess whether this is a safe API change or a broken boundary; if all callers were updated, keep the smaller signature, otherwise use Introduce Parameter Object or preserve the overload until the transition is complete.

java/temporal-review/src/main/java/com/utm/temporal/workflow/PRReviewWorkflowImpl.java @@ -42,12 +42,6 @@ public class PRReviewWorkflowImpl implements PRReviewWorkflow { | "codeQualityActivity.analyze(request);" / "testQualityActivity.analyze(request);" / "securityQualityActivity.analyze(request);" / "complexityQualityActivity.analyze(request);" — The workflow now orchestrates only direct agent calls and no longer applies the removed learning/heuristics layer. Because the diff also deletes the learning activities and database-backed context, this is a broad responsibility shift across the workflow boundary; apply Separate Responsibilities or Extract Workflow/Service so review execution and learning concerns stay decoupled.
The diff introduces new behavior in the PR review comment formatter and changes agent/model defaults, but there are no tests covering the new output structure or the altered fallback behavior.

Test Suggestion 1: Add a unit test for the GitHub Actions comment-generation script that feeds a sample review JSON and asserts the rendered comment includes the verdict banner, summary table, collapsible agent sections, and dashboard link in the expected order.

Test Suggestion 2: Add regression tests for the agent LlmOptions model selection path to verify OPENAI_MODEL overrides the default and that the new hardcoded fallback remains consistent in CodeQuality, Security, TestQuality, Complexity, and Priority agents.

Test Suggestion 3: Add an integration test for PRReviewWorkflowImpl that verifies the final ReviewResponse metadata.model and persisted review output still match the review JSON contract after the agent signature changes and removed learning-related fields.

No security issues detected in the changed code. The only security-relevant logic in `.github/workflows/pr-review.yml` still uses GitHub-provided PR metadata (`github.event.pull_request.*`) and writes review JSON via `jq` without introducing secret exposure, auth bypass, or unsafe input sinks.

The deleted files (`.claude/settings.json`, `.github/workflows/apply-learning.yml`, `docker-compose.learning.yml`, `grafana/*`, `java/temporal-review/src/main/resources/schema.sql`, etc.) remove infrastructure and learning components, but the remaining diff does not add any new logging, hardcoded credentials, or direct use of untrusted input in SQL/command/file-path sinks.

Primary driver: the GitHub Actions comment-generation script now contains multiple mapping objects, helper functions, conditional fallbacks, and two loops that build the comment body and persist review data

P1: No tests cover the new PR review comment formatter output or the changed agent/model fallback behavior, so regressions in rendered comments, model selection, and workflow contract could slip through [TestQuality] - Add unit/integration tests for comment rendering, OPENAI_MODEL override handling, and PRReviewWorkflowImpl output persistence

P2: The OPENAI_MODEL default is duplicated across multiple agents, creating a hidden coupling and drift risk [CodeQuality] - Centralize the model default in a shared constant/config object and pass it through one configuration boundary

P2: The workflow now mixes review execution with removed learning/heuristics responsibilities, increasing boundary complexity [CodeQuality] - Separate workflow orchestration from learning-related concerns or extract a dedicated service

P2: The PR review comment-generation script has moderate complexity with multiple mappings, helpers, and loops, which makes it harder to maintain [Complexity] - Refactor into smaller functions and isolate comment assembly/persistence steps
